title: glaucium grandiflorum subsp. grandiflorum
intro: Glaucium grandiflorum subsp. grandiflorum, also known as the Horned Poppy or Horned Glaucium, is a flowering plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent due to the presence of the alkaloid glaucine. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including rheumatism, fever, and respiratory issues, and have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Glaucium grandiflorum subsp. grandiflorum typically involve the use of its dried flowers, leaves, and roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
